features.description: Investigate existing PR for the mentioned branch
features.details: Check the repository for the existing pull request on branch 'feat/issue-117-manual-review-required-failed-to-process-issue-115'. Document its status, contents, and whether it can be used or should be closed.
Investigate the original issue #115 that started this chain. Understand what the actual work item was and what needs to be implemented or fixed. Document findings.

Based on T001 findings, either: (a) use the existing PR and continue work there, (b) close the existing PR if it's invalid/duplicate, or (c) rename the branch and create a fresh PR. Ensure no work is lost.

features.description: Fix automation workflow to check for existing PRs
features.details: Update the AI automation workflow to check if a PR already exists for a branch before attempting to create a new one. Add proper error handling for this scenario.
Update the automation to use unique branch names (e.g., include timestamp or run ID) to prevent naming collisions when retries occur.

features.description: Close resolved issues in the chain
features.details: Once the actual work is completed, close issues #117 and #122 with appropriate comments linking to the resolution. Ensure issue #115 is properly resolved or closed.

overall_approach: 1) Investigate the existing PR and the branch mentioned in the error, 2) Determine the appropriate resolution (use existing PR, close duplicate, or create new branch), 3) Fix the underlying automation to prevent future duplicate PR attempts, 4) Clean up the chain of related issues (#115, #117, #122).
potential_risks: Losing work that exists in the existing PR if closed improperly
Creating confusion with multiple related issues in the chain
Automation may have other bugs that caused the original issue #115 failure
Branch naming conflicts may recur if automation isn't fixed
